entry.title: The Sydney Morning Herald: 28 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: The Sydney Morning Herald: 28 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-28 07:12AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • WA has its ‘limitations’ but health minister won’t commit to abortion law changes
  • AFL cracks down on ‘exploited’ rule; Ratten says Saints might be too King-focused; Neale re-signs with Lions
  • The Wrap: Commodities boost ASX to fourth day of gains
  • Toll rises after Russian missile strike on Ukrainian shopping centre
  • Murder suspect hospitalised after police shooting
  • Prisoners escape Townsville correctional centre
  • Southern Cross is grim, grimy and depressing. Melbourne deserves better
  • Victims speak following Doncaster aggravated home invasion
  • Questions about the 2022 flu season answered
  • Surprising census results revealed
  • Ex-bikie killed in Sydney shooting
  • Cottesloe council to vote on plan to charge residents for play equipment on verges
  • Rail workers strike causes major delays
  • Thousands of nurses and midwives strike
  • North unleashing a 'hit job': Caro
  • Very little happens, but I didn’t want this train journey to end
  • More than 40 migrants found dead inside truck in Texas
  • Radical North pursuit of Pendlebury drafted
  • Gallery: The best photos from around the world
  • Victoria is more diverse and residents have smaller mortgages, census finds
  • Gen Xers squeezed by their parents, children
  • Albanese’s partner steps onto international stage in Madrid
  • Marles laments ‘drift’ over submarines and frigates as defence chiefs’ terms are extended
  • ‘Welcome to parliament’: The phrase sending a thrill down the spines of new MPs
  • Best of cartoons, June 28, 2022
  • ‘Miss the boat’: Plan to import aged care workers as shortage deepens
  • Teals eye Labor seats, saying Albanese deliberately funded them to fail

  • Caravans, childcare and migration: How COVID changed the nation
  • The latest illustrations from artist Cathy Wilcox
  • The latest illustrations from artist Matt Golding
  • Best of cartoons, June 27, 2022

  • Podcast journalist denies using Hollywood to induce Dawson witnesses
  • COVID-19 booster shots provide greater protection against Omicron in Sydney, data shows
  • ‘Clearly had enemies’: former Lone Wolf bikie dead after Sydney shooting
  • St Catherine’s appoints ‘active Christian’ principal amid same-sex marriage row
  • Climate change protesters Day 2
  • Climate protests and industrial action to slow Sydney’s transport system
  • Callan Park gone to the dogs as off-leash areas set to come into effect
  • Abortion debate without respect will not progress
  • In the Herald: June 28, 1928
  • Police poised to pounce as more disruptive climate protests expected
  • It’s a dog’s afterlife
  • More than half of universities’ overseas enrolments are Chinese students, despite closed borders
  • Protesters swarm the CBD
  • Sydney chiropractor jailed for sexually assaulting Japanese patient
  • Russian missile strikes shopping mall
  • ‘Hot to touch’: Dozens of migrants found dead in semitrailer in Texas
  • Princess Mary pulls son from elite boarding school over culture of abuse
  • Capitol riot panel to hold urgent hearing on additional evidence
  • Rudy Giuliani has man arrested over pat on the back
  • Three killed, dozens hurt in US train crash
  • More than 20 teens found dead in South African nightclub
  • A mystery rocket crashed into the moon – and no one (on Earth) is owning up
  • Toxic gas leak at Jordan port kills more than a dozen, injures hundreds
  • ‘Cataclysmic and chaotic’: Abortion battle lines drawn as legal floodgates open
  • Police officer who shot dead Justine Damond released from prison
  • Albanese says Russia’s ‘brutal’ invasion has major consequences for world
  • At least four dead in Colombian stadium collapse
  • G7 meeting in Germany discusses Ukraine invasion
  • Third day of protests in the US after abortion law overturned
  • Time to enshrine women’s rights into the US constitution
  • ‘Next five years’: Beijing deletes discussion of COVID controls
  • Climate change protesters vow return
  • ‘We don’t accept that happened’: Defence, DVA mishandled complaints
  • Beach campsite rape trial stopped after police delete ‘nude photos’
  • Cashed-up, big houses and urban sprawl: How WA differs from the rest of Australia
  • Australia news LIVE: Census 2021 results released; chaos in Sydney for commuters amid rail industrial action, Blockade Australia protests
  • Former Nuix CEO wanted to sell shares before they fell ‘off a cliff’
  • Jail for bored and ‘staggeringly immature’ Geelong pipebomb maker
  • Print price hike threatens to close Perth’s independent newspapers
  • Why Australian salmon is such a tasty morsel for international buyers
  • KFC to ditch cabbage in ‘five to seven weeks’, CEO says
  • Former Disney boss says streaming is not ‘oversaturated’
  • The billions for Trump’s media deal are under a very heavy cloud
  • Tasmanian salmon grower Tassal rejects $1b Canadian takeover bid
  • Australian businesses offer support to US employees after Roe v Wade
  • Putin will be livid as Russia officially becomes an economic basket case
  • Credit Suisse found guilty in case involving cocaine, cash and a wrestler
  • America’s richest CEOs have a lot to thank Elon Musk for
  • How jobs will save us from a recession
  • Clean energy sector says big batteries, pumped hydro can solve crisis
  • ‘Not running a flower shop’: Star still not suitable to run a casino, inquiry told
  • Trailblazing founder of Rawcus calls time
  • Drugs and death: How can the music industry be saved?
  • These real-life Willy Wonkas are tinkering at the borders of taste
  • Muriel's Wedding trailer
  • Messy humanity of the planet’s saviours: conflict, drama inside XR
  • Deadpan and self-deprecating, Sam Pang is TV’s great disruptor
  • ‘I would like to meet kangaroos’: Hasbulla on touring Australia and the price of fame
  • Succession’s Brian Cox among international stars headed to Australia for festivals
  • ‘Do we have more ideas?’: Chris Hemsworth and Taika Waititi on making a new Thor movie
  • Fat suits are dehumanising, so why do so many actors still wear them?

  • Renewable gas can satisfy our energy hunger
  • Lismore floods by drone
  • Unseasonable rain for Queensland
  • National weather forecast for Tuesday June 28
Breaking news from Sydney, Australia and the world. Features the latest business, sport, entertainment, travel, lifestyle, and technology news.

entry.title: Washington Post: 28 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russia-Ukraine war live updates: Zelensky pushes U.S. to label Russia a terrorism sponsor after strike on Ukrainian mall
  • Chlorine gas leak at port in Jordan kills at least 13, injures hundreds
  • Biden heads to NATO summit on support for Ukraine, alliance strategy
  • Rural rhythms upended by war on the road to Ukraine’s front lines
  • Burials, exhumations, outrage: A day in the endless Philippine drug war
  • At crucial summits, the West squeezes Russia
  • Leonardo Del Vecchio, Italian titan of eyewear, dies at 87
  • At least 15 dead after Russian strike on shopping mall, Ukraine says

  • Exports to Russia from China plummet, study shows
  • NATO to boost high-readiness forces in ‘biggest overhaul’ since Cold War

  • Trump SPAC faces grand jury investigation

  • Credit and home buying: What each generation should think about
  • Each state has its own rules governing estate taxes
  • Three-bedroom townhouse-style condo in Lorton lists below $395,000
  • Ernst & Young IPO Will Create a Lot of Sour Grapes
  • The Lights Are Going Out for Crypto’s Laser-Eyed Grifters
  • Reckless Caution Has Damaged UK Pension Pots
  • Does Naspers’ Tencent Sale Signal China Tech Rally’s End?
  • What Is the ‘Special Debt’ China Uses to Spur Its Economy?
  • A ‘Price Cap’ on Russian Oil — What Would That Mean?

  • Ron DeSantis’s snub of Trump is a 2024 challenge
  • Rudy Giuliani gets caught in another lie — at a supermarket
  • Clarence Thomas hasn’t given up on toppling New York Times v. Sullivan
  • It’s not a court. It’s a junta.
  • The heart of the abortion debate: What is human life?
  • A question for those who say more faith will prevent gun violence: How?
  • With the world distracted, Cuba cracks down on dissident artists
  • Joe Manchin is quietly pushing Democrats into another catastrophe
  • What the Nixon pardon tells us about the perils of letting Trump walk
  • Search and body seizure
  • Virginia’s top health official needs an education on racism

  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more

  • Contact the reader representative

Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

The Korea JoongAng Daily is an English-language daily published by the JoongAng Group, Korea’s leading media group, in association with The New York Times.

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 28 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 28 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-28 06:29AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • No-fly zone above high-speed rail terminus part of area lockdown, as Hong Kong rolls out security blanket for Xi Jinping’s visit to mark 25th anniversary of handover

  • Hong Kong is a city of duelling narratives, but deep down we know the truth
  • Hong Kong’s greenhouse gas emissions fall for third year, but concern groups say more work needed
  • Hong Kong police arrest 6 men in connection with predawn shooting in Central; 3 others detained in separate firearm cases
  • Power company to remove remains of bridge and finish laying cables a week after fire that left 160,000 Hong Kong households without electricity
  • Number of coronavirus cases linked to Hong Kong nursing homes rises as health experts call for unvaccinated elderly residents to get jabs
  • HK Express flight makes emergency landing at Hong Kong airport after report of smoke in cockpit, all 47 passengers and 7 crew members unharmed
  • Evergrande crisis: embattled developer could face liquidation as creditor files winding-up petition in Hong Kong
  • China’s H-6 bombers in show of air-navy coordination near southern Japan, military experts say
  • Xi Jinping points to public opinion campaign as example of ‘whole-process democracy’
  • China’s yuan liquidity reserve pool highlights efforts to loosen US dollar hegemony
  • US and Taiwan hold first round of trade talks in new bid to counter China’s economic influence
  • US urged to plan minelaying campaign to halt mainland Chinese attack on Taiwan
  • Joe Biden swipes at China, signing pledge to combat illegal fishing
  • US-led rare earths pact satisfies South Korea’s ‘definite need’ to cut China dependency
  • Belgium prime minister cautions against treating China as if it is another Russia
  • G7 summit statement will take aim at ‘challenge China poses’, US national security adviser says
  • A thaw in China-US diplomatic relations? We can only hope
  • China hits back at US over G7 ‘zero-sum’ belt and road alternative
  • JD.com founder Richard Liu cashes out nearly US$1 billion from e-commerce giant after retreat from top job
  • Cryptocurrency is ‘biggest Ponzi scheme in human history’, Chinese execs from state-backed blockchain drive say
  • Taiwan, US to hold first trade talks under new initiative
  • China’s May industrial profits kept tumbling, raising calls for more government support
  • China to extend yuan trading hours in bid to boost global use of its currency
  • Tuvalu minister pulls out of UN Ocean Conference after China blocks its Taiwanese delegates
  • ByteDance’s metaverse investments continue with acquisition of Chinese virtual social platform PoliQ
  • Hong Kong star Jackie Chan attracts 130 million on Kuaishou as Chinese platforms deploy celebrity live-streams to lure users
  • Chinese prototype a precursor to hypersonic passenger travel anywhere in the world in 2 hours, says team
  • Hong Kong adjusts to new economic role after blazing trail for others
  • Thailand tops 2 million tourists this year, as it longs for absent Chinese visitors
  • Australia-China relations: Canberra to set up Pacific defence school as Beijing seeks rival regional meeting
  • Singapore man who preyed on disabled children jailed for record 45 years in ‘exceptionally sickening’ case
  • Malaysian immigration slammed over deaths of 149 Indonesians: ‘No clean water, atrocious food’
  • Indonesia’s Jokowi likely to talk food prices with Putin even as he calls for ‘peace’ amid Ukraine war
  • Asean special envoy appeals to Myanmar junta to spare Suu Kyi prison
  • Singapore’s return to tighter Covid rules cannot be ruled out, deputy PM Wong says
  • South Korean Navy Seal broke the law to fight in Ukraine, calls it ‘the Wild West’
  • Japan’s snub of talks with South Korea on sidelines of Nato summit a ‘missed opportunity’
  • Is the US setting up an ‘Asia-style Nato’? North Korea thinks so
  • ‘Died as they danced’: Speculation swirls in South Africa over tragic teen party
  • Accused ‘duck sauce killer’ of Chinese food delivery worker in New York City released on US$500,000 bail
  • US commerce secretary urges lawmakers to pass US$52 billion chip-making bill, warning companies could turn elsewhere
  • Tragedy in Texas as 46 dead migrants found in sweltering truck trailer
  • Chlorine gas blast at Jordan port kills at least 12, injures over 250
  • How an expanded BRICS could lead the world instead of the waning West
  • FBI agents seize phone of Donald Trump’s election lawyer
  • 3 killed as Amtrak train hits truck, derails in Missouri
  • US man shoots Subway worker dead over ‘too much mayo on sandwich’
  • At least 18 killed in Russian missile strike on crowded Ukraine shopping centre
  • Britain ‘sick man of Europe’ is hurtling towards breaking point after being battered by Brexit and Covid-19
  • Nato to boost rapid reaction force in ‘biggest overhaul since Cold War’
  • Russia to spend US$14.5 billion on aircraft amid sanctions
  • Ukraine: ‘Help us end war by winter’, Zelensky urges world leaders – as Russia pours fire on stronghold
  • Chinese spicy snack giant Weilong Delicious seeks to relaunch Hong Kong IPO targeting US$500 million, says source
  • What does gloomy US and China economic outlook mean for stock prices?
  • Why Hong Kong landlords, developers and asset managers cannot ignore ESG standards
  • Hong Kong stocks retreat from 3-month high as Tencent, JD.com lead losses after selling by major insiders
  • Taiwan’s GlobalWafers plans US$5 billion silicon wafer plant in Texas after failed European investment
  • Rooftop farms in Hong Kong skyscrapers provide fruitful and nutritious hobby to stressed-out urbanites
  • Use of bitcoin, other digital money to buy property grinds to halt as cryptocurrency market crashes
  • Chinese stocks’ US$1.8 trillion recovery from April low faces another earnings test as UBS, Citic predict setbacks
  • Hong Kong, Singapore investors filling void in London property left by mainland Chinese retreat
  • Beijing and Wuhan join local Chinese governments to bolster EV sales by offering subsidies to buyers
  • Qianhai offers cheap housing and full-time jobs in bid to attract more Hongkongers to work in special economic zone
  • Tencent takes a US$7.4 billion market knock as Prosus prepares to cut stake to fund stock buy-back, sells JD.com as non-strategic asset
  • Britain’s pensions agency to remove Chinese-made security cameras, ban new purchases
  • China needs to act fast to close the widening supply-demand gap for low-carbon talent, experts say
  • Hong Kong can be a hub, not just for capital going into China, but also funds coming out of mainland for M&A, ex-SFC chairman says
  • Korean soup, stews and broths: all you need to know about the dishes that represent the very lifeblood of a nation
  • Alcohol-free drinks events a growing trend, drawing the sober curious and health-focused people
  • Hong Kong singer Mike Tsang on performing with George Lam, his new album, and who he would love to collaborate with
  • London’s Regent Street struggles to draw shoppers back after pandemic, amid rise in online retail
  • Minions: The Rise of Gru movie review – Steve Carell, Michelle Yeoh and Jean-Claude Van Damme enliven enjoyable second prequel to Despicable Me
  • The 10 best K-dramas of 2022 … so far, from Our Blues and Link: Eat, Love, Kill to Money Heist: Korea – Joint Economic Area
  • Who are Lionesses? Queer K-pop boy band who want to be the LGBTQ+ icons they wish they’d had growing up
  • Pandemic anger, ‘panger’, is a real mental health concern. Expert suggests 4 steps to deal with your emotions
  • Hermès brought summer holiday vibes to Paris Fashion Week with pastel colours and a contemporary take on Birkin’s ancestor – the Haut à courroies travel bag – for its menswear spring/summer 2023 show
  • Meet Chinese billionaire Weili Dai, one of the US’s richest self-made women: the Shanghai-born ‘technology geek’ made her money through semiconductors, joining Kim K and Rihanna on Forbes’ 2022 list
  • STYLE Edit: Omega releases the newest Mini Trésor collection in celebration of its star-studded campaign, featuring inspiring women including Nicole Kidman, Zoë Kravitz, Kaia Gerber and Zhou Dongyu
  • Meet Muhammad Ali’s grandson, Biaggio Ali Walsh: unlike the boxing legend, the American footballer debuted with his first MMA match this month, and once modelled for Versace with Irina Shayk
  • 10 richest golf stars of 2022 – net worths, ranked: Tiger Woods is the sport’s first billionaire, but how do Rory McIlroy, Jordan Spieth and Phil Mickelson tee up?
  • Inside Kim Kardashian’s US$150 million private jet, aka Kim Air: the cashmere-clad Gulfstream plane seats 18 and is pricier than even Jeff Bezos and Elon Musk’s rides
  • Meet Cy Busson, heartthrob son of supermodel Elle Macpherson and billionaire Arpad Busson: he’s already appeared on Vogue’s cover, has PDA snaps on Instagram and loves travelling
  • Virgil Abloh’s legacy at Louis Vuitton’s Paris Fashion Week menswear show: the bold spring/summer 2023 collection is the fashion house’s first not created by the late designer
  • 8 best modern Chinese restaurants in Hong Kong: from favourites Man Ho and Mott 32, to celebrity chef Vicky Cheng’s Wing and fine dining Cantonese at Rùn
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: Financial Times: 28 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • G7 ready to explore caps on energy prices to curb Russian revenues
  • ExxonMobil chief predicts continuing surge in oil markets
  • Why the US could be the real winner in the energy wars
  • The surprising revival of eastern Germany
  • Code red: China’s Covid health apps govern life but are ripe for abuse

  • Foreign business in Hong Kong looks to the long term

  • Sri Lanka halts fuel supplies for private vehicles
  • Stress tests drive higher capital requirements at 3 biggest US banks
  • Plural launches €250mn entrepreneur-led fund for European tech start-ups

  • Abortion pills in demand after reversal of Roe vs Wade

  • Iranian hardliners back Raisi’s low-key approach despite protests

  • Trump’s plans for Spac deal suffer further blow after subpoenas
  • Russian espionage ‘booming’ in Switzerland, intelligence chiefs warn
  • London’s Heathrow airport forced to cut landing charges
  • Cheap bonds! Sort of!

  • FirstFT: G7 explores energy price caps
  • Young men are slipping quietly through the economy’s cracks
  • UK start-up secures funding for one of Europe’s first lithium refineries
  • Four of London’s most offbeat public tennis courts
  • Steel yourself: UK trade policy is suffering post-Brexit drift
  • Why the inflation boost to UK public finances is unlikely to last
  • New ‘no-fault’ divorce laws may stimulate rise in amicable splits among the wealthy
  • The cylindrical home where the architectural styles of Victorian London and postwar council housing coalesce
  • Nato must show it is serious about defending its eastern flank
  • US Supreme Court finds for high-school coach in religious freedom case
  • Abortion providers are doing all we can — but we can only do so much
  • Food deserts deepen urban America’s racial disparities
  • America alone on abortion
  • Tech’s uninspiring hiring and firing
  • Big Tech: bloated overheads mean earnings risk ahead
  • Prosus/Tencent: reducing stake further would close valuation gap

  • Naspers to sell more of Tencent stake in bid to revive shares
  • What can the past tell us about Bitcoin’s future?
  • Smart beta ETF launches sink to 12-year low
  • US stocks struggle for direction as traders reassess rate rise outlook
  • Revlon/meme stocks: continued speculation hints that bear market is not over
  • Boris Johnson prepares to extend tariffs to protect UK steel industry
  • Russia/sanctions: G7 seeks more damage to the war machine, less to its own economies
  • France’s approval of crypto exchange Binance branded ‘incomprehensible’ by MEP
  • Ukraine’s richest oligarch launches lawsuit against Russia
  • A groundbreaking argument in climate litigation
  • Russia set for first debt default since 1998 as payment deadline passes
  • Lithium producer Lake Resources downplays short selling bonanza
  • Japan tells business and public to save power to avert Tokyo blackout
  • Giannis Antetokounmpo and the making of a legend
  • Can we avoid disasters, and should we even bother trying?
  • London: global city status is hard to burnish by prescription
Financial Times

entry.title: ChannelNewsAsia: 28 Jun, 2022
entry.content:

entry.title: Asahi Shimbun: 28 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:

  • G7 aims to raise $600 billion to counter China's Belt and Road

  • VOX POPULI: Looking toward Australia to find ways to entice Japanese to vote
The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.

entry.title: NY Times (Print): 28 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Supreme Court Sides With Coach Over Prayers at the 50-Yard Line
